rationale_for_baseline_tier: Sudden severe crushing central chest pain at rest radiating to the arm and jaw, with profuse diaphoresis, resting dyspnoea and a sense of impending doom, is a suspected acute myocardial infarction — an immediate threat to life. There is no home reperfusion, ECG or troponin, and cardiac arrest can occur at any moment. T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance within minutes) is the only appropriate tier; the semi-rural location and longer ambulance response time reinforce calling immediately rather than delaying.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Ongoing severe crushing chest pain at rest with radiation, diaphoresis and breathlessness
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 now — within minutes; do not wait to see if it settles
escalation_edges.rationale: Suspected acute coronary syndrome / myocardial infarction requires emergency ambulance transport for ECG, troponin and reperfusion — time is myocardium.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This could be a heart attack. I'm going to help you call an ambulance on triple zero (000) right now. Stay sitting and still while we do — do not walk around or drive.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of consciousness or cessation of normal breathing before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — begin CPR and follow the 000 dispatcher's instructions
escalation_edges.rationale: ACS can precipitate a malignant arrhythmia and cardiac arrest; immediate bystander CPR is the single greatest determinant of survival.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes unresponsive or stops breathing normally, lie him flat on the floor and start pushing hard and fast in the centre of his chest — the 000 operator will count you through it. Don't hang up.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any temptation to self-transport or delay for a GP/next-day review
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Do not self-transport at any point — wait for the ambulance
escalation_edges.rationale: Self-driving during an evolving infarct risks collapse at the wheel and forfeits pre-hospital ECG, defibrillation capability and the cardiology pathway.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Please don't drive yourselves or wait for the doctor tomorrow — the ambulance brings the heart monitor and treatment to you, and it's far safer.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives on the semi-rural fringe with a longer expected ambulance response time
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Call 000 immediately and without any hesitation — the longer transport interval makes early activation and continuous monitoring of the patient even more important; keep the front door unlocked and someone watching for the ambulance.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them it is a suspected heart attack with severe central chest pain. Keep the patient sitting upright and completely at rest — do not let him stand, walk or drive. If aspirin is in the house and he is not allergic to it and has no active bleeding, have him chew a 300 mg tablet. Stay with him, keep the front door unlocked, and if he collapses or stops breathing normally start CPR with the 000 operator guiding you.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or an after-hours GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary with the ECG, troponin results and any reperfusion performed — take these to the GP for secondary-prevention and cardiac-rehabilitation follow-up.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating suspected ACS as reflux, muscle strain or a non-urgent problem and arranging self-care or a routine/same-day GP review instead of an ambulance.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Untreated myocardial infarction progresses to malignant arrhythmia and death, or large irreversible myocardial necrosis, before the patient reaches definitive care.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising the patient to get to an emergency department by their own means rather than dispatching an ambulance.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: T5 is the correct tier here, so there is no meaningful over-triage; erring toward 000 for chest pain of this character is always appropriate.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact cardiac diagnosis (STEMI vs NSTEMI vs unstable angina — or an alternative such as PE) cannot be determined without an ECG and troponin, none of which are available via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the worst plausible cause: treat as a suspected heart attack, call 000 immediately, give early aspirin if safe, and let the hospital resolve the diagnosis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the diagnostic uncertainty as a reason to wait, observe at home, or default to a benign explanation such as reflux.
